examples.title: Simple Base-Limit Address Translation
examples.description: Demonstrates logical to physical address translation using base register with bounds checking
examples.code:
// Address Translation with Protection
INPUT: logical_address, base_register, limit_register
OUTPUT: physical_address or TRAP

BEGIN
  // Check if address is within bounds
  IF logical_address < 0 OR logical_address >= limit_register THEN
    TRAP // Memory protection fault
    RETURN -1
  ENDIF
  
  // Translate logical to physical address
  physical_address = base_register + logical_address
  RETURN physical_address
END
examples.explanation: Each process has its own base and limit register. The CPU generates logical addresses which are translated by adding the base register value. The limit register ensures the address is within the process's allocated memory region.

examples.title: First Fit Memory Allocation
examples.description: Allocates memory using first fit strategy in variable-sized partitions
examples.code:
// First Fit Memory Allocation
INPUT: process_size
OUTPUT: allocated_address or NULL

VARIABLES: memory_map, hole

BEGIN
  FOR each hole IN memory_map DO
    IF hole.size >= process_size THEN
      // Allocate at start of hole
      allocated_address = hole.start
      
      // If hole is larger than needed, create new smaller hole
      IF hole.size > process_size THEN
        new_hole.start = allocated_address + process_size
        new_hole.size = hole.size - process_size
        INSERT new_hole INTO memory_map
      ENDIF
      
      // Reduce original hole size
      REMOVE hole FROM memory_map
      RETURN allocated_address
    ENDIF
  ENDFOR
  
  RETURN NULL // No suitable hole found
END
examples.explanation: Scans memory list from beginning and allocates the first hole large enough to fit the process. After allocation, if there's remaining space, it creates a smaller hole for future use.

examples.title: Memory Management API in C
examples.description: Demonstrates basic memory allocation and deallocation functions
examples.explanation: Demonstrates C memory management functions: malloc for allocation, realloc for resizing, and free for deallocation. These are low-level memory management primitives used in system programming.
examples.timeComplexity: O(n) for realloc, O(1) for malloc/free (amortized)

examples.title: Simulated Memory Allocation Tracker
examples.description: Python class demonstrating basic memory allocation tracking
examples.explanation: Python implementation of a simple memory manager demonstrating first-fit allocation, tracking allocated blocks and free holes, and handling deallocation with coalescing of adjacent free blocks.
examples.timeComplexity: O(n) for allocation, O(1) for deallocation
